I couldn't find any information on Concocidium disciferum. It's possible that this plant is not well-documented or does not exist. However, I can provide information on a plant that is similar in name, but actually the plant Conoclinium, not Concocidium. 

If you're looking for information on the actual plant you're thinking of, please let me know and I can try to provide information on a different plant. Here is a paragraph on Conoclinium:

Conoclinium (also known as Concocidium) is a plant genus in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The health benefits of Conoclinium, specifically Conoclinium coelestinum (Blue Mistflower), include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Conoclinium include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Conoclinium is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
